topics: Introduction to cryogenic engineering: historical background and major events in the development of cryogenic engineering
Low temperature properties of engineering materials: mechanical, thermal, electric, and magnetic properties
Cryogenic fluids (N2, CH4) and their properties
Applications of cryogenics: space, food processing, superconductivity, electrical power, biology, medicine, electronics, quantum computing, and manufacturing industry
Cryogenic liquefaction systems: system performance parameters, ideal liquefaction system, Joule-Thomson expansion, and adiabatic expansion
Liquefaction systems for gases other than Neon, Hydrogen and Helium: simple Linde-Hampson system, Claude, cascade system, and auto-cascade system
Liquefaction systems for Neon, Hydrogen and Helium: LN2 precooled Linde-Hampson and Claude systems
Ortho to para conversion arrangement in hydrogen liquefaction system
Simon helium liquefaction system and Collins helium liquefaction system
Critical components of liquefaction systems: critical components, types of heat exchanger used in cryogenic systems and their effect on system performance
Cryogenic refrigeration systems: ideal isothermal and isobaric refrigeration systems
Refrigeration using liquids as refrigerant: Linde-Hampson refrigerator and Claude refrigerator

Refrigeration using gases as refrigerant: Stirling cycle cryocoolers, Philips refrigerator, effect of regenerator effectiveness on performance of the Philips refrigerator, Gifford McMahon refrigerators, and pulse tube cryocoolers
Refrigerators using solids as working media: magnetic refrigerators, thermodynamics of magnetic refrigerators, and dilution refrigerators
Cryogenic fluid storage and transfer systems: cryogenic fluid storage vessel
Thermal insulations and their performance at cryogenic temperatures: super insulations, vacuum insulation, and powder insulation
Cryogenic fluid transfer systems and cryo pumping
Cryogenic instrumentation
Pressure measurement: McLeod gauge, Pirani gauge, and Penning gauge
Flow measurement: orifice meter, venturi meter, and turbine flow meter
Liquid level gauges: hydrostatic, resistance gauge, capacitance gauge, and thermodynamic gauge
Temperature measurements: ITS-90, thermocouple, magnetic thermometers, and vapor pressure thermometers
Safety in cryogenic fluid handling, storage, and use
